title: A festival I watched from the outside
summary: Narrate a local festival or celebration you observed as a visitor who did not fully understand it.
topic: A festival I watched as a visitor
prompt: Write 8–10 sentences about a local festival or celebration you saw but were not part of. Describe what you saw, heard, and felt. Use past simple. Include at least one thing you did not understand.
hintStarters: I arrived in the town on the day of the festival, without knowing that…
The streets were suddenly full of people wearing…
I stood near the edge of the crowd and watched…
I did not understand what the ceremony meant, but I felt…

hintVocab.meaningVi: trang phục truyền thống, hóa trang
di chuyển qua, len lỏi qua

sampleText: I arrived in Oaxaca, Mexico, on the night of the Day of the Dead celebrations, without knowing what I was walking into. The streets were full of people in white face paint and long dark costumes who moved slowly through the town. Some of them carried large candles and others carried photos of people I guessed were family members who had died. There was a sound of quiet drums and a low, repeated chant that I did not recognise. I stood at the edge of the crowd and watched, feeling very much like an outsider. A small girl saw me standing alone and pulled her mother's sleeve. Her mother turned, smiled at me, and waved me a little closer. I moved forward and stayed there for almost an hour. I did not understand the ceremony, but I understood that it was something serious and full of love. At some point, someone handed me a marigold and I held it without knowing exactly what to do with it. Later, I sat in a café and asked the owner what the flower meant. She explained that marigolds guide the dead back home. That one small sentence changed the way I remembered the whole evening.

sampleAnnotations.phrase: without knowing what I was walking into
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: mà không biết mình đang bước vào điều gì
di chuyển chậm rãi qua thị trấn
tiếng tụng kinh thấp, lặp đi lặp lại
vẫy tôi lại gần hơn một chút

sampleAnnotations.phrase: feeling very much like an outsider
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: cảm thấy rất rõ như một người ngoài cuộc

sampleAnnotations.phrase: something serious and full of love
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: điều gì đó nghiêm trang và đầy tình yêu thương
đưa cho tôi một bông cúc vạn thọ

sampleAnnotations.phrase: without knowing exactly what to do with it
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: mà không biết chính xác phải làm gì với nó
dẫn đường cho người đã khuất trở về nhà
thay đổi cách tôi nhớ lại

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Past simple for completed narrative events
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Mỗi hành động trong câu chuyện dùng past simple: 'I arrived', 'She smiled', 'I moved', 'Someone handed'. Đây là thì chính để kể chuyện trong quá khứ khi mỗi sự kiện đã kết thúc.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: Each action in the story uses past simple: 'I arrived', 'She smiled', 'I moved', 'Someone handed'. This is the main tense for storytelling in the past when each event is finished.

sampleTranslationVi: Tôi đến Oaxaca, Mexico, vào đêm lễ hội Ngày của Người Chết, mà không biết mình đang bước vào điều gì. Các con phố đầy người mặc trang phục tối màu dài và mặt sơn trắng, họ di chuyển chậm rãi qua thị trấn. Một số người cầm những cây nến lớn, và một số khác cầm ảnh của những người mà tôi đoán là thành viên gia đình đã qua đời. Có tiếng trống nhỏ và một tiếng tụng kinh thấp, lặp đi lặp lại mà tôi không nhận ra. Tôi đứng ở rìa đám đông và nhìn, cảm thấy rất rõ như một người ngoài cuộc. Một cô bé nhỏ thấy tôi đứng một mình và kéo tay áo mẹ. Mẹ cô quay lại, mỉm cười với tôi, và vẫy tôi lại gần hơn một chút. Tôi tiến lên phía trước và ở đó gần một tiếng đồng hồ. Tôi không hiểu nghi lễ đó, nhưng tôi hiểu rằng đó là điều gì đó nghiêm trang và đầy tình yêu thương. Tại một thời điểm nào đó, có người đưa cho tôi một bông cúc vạn thọ và tôi cầm nó mà không biết chính xác phải làm gì với nó. Sau đó, tôi ngồi trong một quán cà phê và hỏi chủ quán ý nghĩa của bông hoa. Bà giải thích rằng cúc vạn thọ dẫn đường cho người đã khuất trở về nhà. Điều đó thay đổi cách tôi nhớ lại cả buổi tối.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct way to begin a story about arriving unexpectedly during an event.
options: I arrived without knowing what I walk into.
I arrived without knowing what I was walking into.
I arrived without knowing what I walked into.
I arrived without knowing what I am walking into.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Without knowing what I was walking into' uses past continuous to show the ongoing situation at the moment of arrival. The continuous is more natural here than simple past.
mcQuestions.hint: What was happening at the moment you arrived — an ongoing situation or a single completed action?

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the best phrase to describe standing at the side of a large crowd.
options: at the end of the crowd
at the edge of the crowd
at the side of crowd
at the border of the crowd
mcQuestions.explanation: 'At the edge of the crowd' is the natural phrase for being just outside a group of people.
mcQuestions.hint: Which fixed phrase describes being just outside a group?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly uses 'hand' as a verb meaning to give something?
options: Someone handed me a marigold.
Someone handed to me a marigold.
Someone handded me a marigold.
Someone hand me a marigold.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Hand someone something' (no preposition) is the correct double-object construction. 'Handed to me' is less standard; 'handded' is a misspelling; 'hand' is present tense.
mcQuestions.hint: Does 'hand' (give) take a preposition before the person?

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the sentence that correctly uses 'explain' to report information.
options: She explained me that marigolds guide the dead back home.
She explained that marigolds guide the dead back home.
She explained to that marigolds guide the dead back home.
She explained me marigolds guide the dead back home.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Explain that + clause' does not take an indirect object directly. 'She explained to me that…' is correct if a person is named.
mcQuestions.hint: Does 'explain' take a direct object (a person) before the clause, or not?

mcQuestions.prompt: Select the sentence that uses 'without' correctly with a verb.
options: I held the flower without to know what to do.
I held the flower without knowing what to do.
I held the flower without know what to do.
I held the flower without I knowing what to do.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Without + verb-ing' is the correct structure. 'Without' is a preposition and is followed by a gerund (-ing form).
mcQuestions.hint: What form of the verb follows a preposition like 'without'?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly says that something changed your perspective on a past event?
options: That one small sentence changed the way I remembered the whole evening.
That changed the way I was remembering the whole evening.
That changed the way I remember the whole evening.
That has changed the way I remembered the whole evening.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Changed the way I remembered' keeps both verbs in past simple, consistent with the completed past narrative.
mcQuestions.hint: In a completed past story, what tense should both verbs be?

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the correct description of someone who is not part of a local event.
options: I felt like an outsider.
I felt like an outside.
I felt like an outstander.
I felt like a foreigner person.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Outsider' is the correct noun for someone who is not part of a group. 'Outside' is not used as a noun for people; 'outstander' is not a word; 'foreigner person' is redundant.
mcQuestions.hint: Which word means 'a person who is not part of a group'?

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct word for the repeated singing or speaking in a religious ceremony.
options: drum
float
chant
lantern
mcQuestions.explanation: A 'chant' is a repeated phrase or melody sung in a ceremony. A 'drum' is a percussion instrument; a 'float' is a decorated vehicle in a parade; a 'lantern' is a light.
mcQuestions.hint: Which word describes repeated singing or speaking in a ceremony?

mcQuestions.prompt: Select the sentence where 'guessed' is used correctly.
options: I guessed were family members who had died.
I guessed that they were family members who had died.
I guessed they were family members who died.
I guessed they were family members who had died.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'I guessed they were family members who had died' is correct. Past perfect 'had died' shows those people died before the story moment. Option B adds 'that' which is also correct, making A and D both potentially valid — but D is the most natural spoken form.
mcQuestions.hint: Do the family members' deaths happen before or after the story moment?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence best describes moving forward in a crowd?
options: I moved forward and stayed there for almost an hour.
I moved forwards and stayed there during almost an hour.
I moved ahead and stayed there since almost an hour.
I moved forward and stayed there for nearly an hour.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'For almost an hour' is correct for duration. 'During' needs a noun event; 'since' marks a start point. Option D ('for nearly an hour') is also correct, but option A matches the sample.
mcQuestions.hint: Which preposition shows how long you stayed somewhere?

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct question form to ask what a flower means.
options: I asked what does the flower mean.
I asked what the flower meant.
I asked what the flower means.
I asked what the flower was meaning.
mcQuestions.explanation: In indirect questions (after 'asked'), use statement word order: 'what the flower meant', not 'what does the flower mean'.
mcQuestions.hint: In a reported question, does the word order stay like a direct question?

criticalThinkingQuestion: Have you ever watched a ceremony or celebration you did not fully understand? Did not understanding it make the experience more or less meaningful for you?
